---
title: "File Formats"
description: "Status of OPALX input, field-map, distribution, diagnostic, and statistics formats."
audience: user
status: planned
last-reviewed: "2026-07-22"
---

OPALX consumes statement-oriented `.in` files plus model-specific field maps
and particle distributions. It produces statistics, particle, field, loss,
and diagnostic data through several writer paths.

This reference remains **planned** until each format is traced to its current
OPALX reader or writer. The historical OPAL storage-format pages are not copied
here because they may describe fields, ordering, or compatibility behavior
that OPALX no longer guarantees.

Each completed format page will specify:

- File extension and text or binary encoding.
- Units, coordinate frame, and column ordering.
- Required and optional metadata.
- Parallel write behavior.
- Versioning and backward-compatibility policy.
- A minimal reader and writer example.
